author: zachmueller
description: A transparent, extensible, autonomous-capable AI assistant for note taking and knowledge management. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Zach Mueller
manifest.description: A transparent, extensible, autonomous-capable AI assistant for note taking and knowledge management.
manifest.name: Notor

name: Notor

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Spreadsheet tools** — Four new ExcelJS-backed built-in tools: `read_xlsx`, `write_xlsx`, `import_xlsx`, and `list_xlsx_sheets`. Read sheets as Markdown or JSON with sheet selection and size caps, write from Markdown tables or JSON, and import filesystem workbooks into vault notes. `exceljs` is now available to custom tools via `libs.exceljs`.
- **Section-header references in chat** — After picking a note with `[[`, an Obsidian-style section picker opens so you can append a heading, producing `[[Note § Heading]]` attachments. Pasted `[[wikilinks]]` in chat text are auto-detected and spliced in as inline attachment tokens.
- **Custom request parameters for local LLM providers** — Attach a free-form JSON object (e.g. Ollama `keep_alive`) to a local provider config; it merges into each chat-completions request. Includes inline validation that rejects malformed or non-object JSON.
- **Interactive tools exempt from timeout auto-cancel** — Tools that await user input (e.g. `ask_user`) no longer get auto-cancelled by the execution timeout when a live chat channel is present. New `auto_skip_user_input_prompts` setting (default off) to opt back into auto-skipping stale prompts.

### Reliability — Model Selection, Thinking & Bedrock
- **Unified model/thinking state** across new chats, forks, and reopened conversations — `thinking_level` is now persisted on the conversation header and restored on fork/reopen, eliminating the "screwy" model selector divergence where surfaces could disagree.
- **Single source of truth for thinking capability** — `getThinkingCapability` classifies each model identically across every code path; unknown/unsupported models correctly send no thinking payload.
- **Bedrock data-retention error detection** — The Bedrock "data retention mode" error is now caught and surfaced with an actionable message naming the account-level fix. Provider errors are persisted into conversation history so failed chats are diagnosable on reload and render in replay and HTML/Markdown exports.

### Bug Fixes
- **Long-note-write recovery** — When a long write tool call is truncated, the target path is now recovered from the partial JSON and the error message includes skeleton-first steering (write skeleton → replace per section → update frontmatter). Partial content is never re-entered as a successful write.
- **Tab completion in the note suggester** now selects the highlighted item (via Obsidian's own selection controller) instead of always resolving to the first item. Enter and mouse clicks finalize the plain `[[Note]]`; Tab opens the section picker.
- **Note attachment display** — Removed `.md` from vault-note token/chip display; attachment content is stored as per-attachment snapshots (text for notes, content-hash re-resolve for vault media, base64 for external media) with metadata-aware token estimation.

### Internal / Build
- Hardened the esbuild script-injection strip against dependency drift (exact-pinned `docx`/`mammoth`, `overrides` for `jszip`/`immediate`/`setimmediate`/`lie`) with a dedicated test suite, and fixed residual blocking lint errors from the plugin-review pass.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- **Orchestration recovery:** Clicking **Dismiss** on the "Orchestration '…' was interrupted. Resume it?" notice now persists your decision. Previously Dismiss only closed the toast, so the same notices reappeared on every Obsidian restart/reload. Dismissing now marks the session as cancelled (a terminal state), so the load-time recovery scan no longer re-offers it.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes
- **Stop control for orchestration runs** — a run registry now tracks live runs, exposes a Stop button in the UI, aborts cleanly, and tears down on plugin unload.
- **Code-step visibility** — code steps and their logs now surface in the Run tree, and an `orchestration.log` captures code-step output.
- **Runtime API v1 handshake** — extensions can declare a minimum API version (`notor-min-api`) and read `utils.api.version` for forward-compatible behavior.
- Fixed an inert Stop button on stale or background orchestration runs.
- Honest stop reasons: stream errors now route to a dedicated failure channel, and errored/cancelled turns are reported accurately instead of as success.
- Corrected wind-down, errored-turn, and child-cost accounting.
- Made history and session writes atomic (remove-before-rename) to prevent corruption on overwrite.
- `emit_event` is now classified as a read operation so it is no longer blocked in Plan mode.
- Manual workflow chats now stay attached to the active conversation.
- Child-flow ledger entries are matched by (step, flow, ordinal) for reliable recovery.

### Reliability & Safety
- Added a per-flow single-instance guard and a recovery liveness guard with offered resume and schedule-origin recovery.
- Added execution timeouts for tools and automations via a shared `withTimeout`.
- All six persisted formats now carry a `schema_version` stamp with append conversion.
- Gated `utils.webview` on the webview tool being enabled; narrowed live-manager utils members to safer facades.

### Internal / Architecture
- Guided models to stage large tool-parameter payloads.
- Decomposed `launch.ts` into focused responsibility modules behind a thin barrel; introduced `OrchestrationHost`; lazy-loaded orchestration layers.
- Unified tool-policy evaluation into a single superset path (`evaluateToolPolicy`) and removed the legacy branch; threaded `policyCtx` through the run loop, sub-agents, and orchestration.
- Extensive new test coverage (unit + live e2e) across all six architecture findings.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features

- **Orchestration engine.** Define multi-step flows that chain LLM turns, programmatic code steps, and nested sub-flows into automated, repeatable workflows. Highlights:
  - Flow schema and core execution engine built on a generalized RunLoop substrate
  - Session workspace, task tracking, crash recovery, and step edges
  - Programmatic **code steps** for deterministic logic between LLM turns
  - **Flow composition** — flows can invoke child flows via `run_flow`, with cascading guardrails and child-run metadata
  - **Interactive pause** and step→workflow hand-off
  - Per-turn progress **Notices** so long-running flows report status as they go
  - **Direct scheduling** so flows can be launched on a schedule (and via hooks)
  - Unified **run-tree view** with an inline peek card and typed activity entries
  - New **orchestration-creator** persona plus reference example flows to get started
- **Fork & re-run tool** conversation fork mode.
- Added a toggle controlling whether orchestrations open their notes in the editor.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **OS-level desktop notifications** for chat completion and input-required events
- **`word_count` built-in tool** (optional) for counting words in notes
- **`delete_note` built-in tool** (optional, disabled by default)
- **Multi-select (checkbox) answers** in the `ask_user` tool, alongside the existing single-select
- **Backlinks context** can now optionally be included in `read_note` results
- **`conversationApi.current()`** — a parse-free metadata accessor for extensions

### Workflows
- Active-workflow **chip** with a one-click deactivate action
- **Switch a workflow into the current conversation** without starting a new chat
- Workflow tool configs now **persist across follow-up turns**
- Workflow chip refreshes when a workflow spins up a new conversation
- Stranded background workflows are now **cleared on system sleep/wake**

### Tooling & Reliability
- **Tiered resilient matching** for the replace tools, with clearer no-op-edit warnings and a configurable full-content-failure response
- Renamed `replace_in_note`/`replace_in_file` params `search`/`replace` → `old_text`/`new_text`
- Clear error surfaced for an invalid `execute_command` working directory
- AI task list now **persists to the JSONL header** and survives reopening
- Partial tool-call content is **preserved when a long write is cut off**

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed **Bedrock orphaned-`tool_result` error** via pair-aware truncation and wire repair
- Fixed `replace_in_note` diff-approval crash and hang on malformed changes
- Fixed the sleep auto-approve default via a shared mode-aware helper

### UI Polish
- Render **in-progress tool-call cards** during streaming
- Task panel auto-scrolls to the next unchecked task
- Show the model name in the collapsed model-selector dropdown
- Highlight tool-search matches and tighten filtering
- Refined `ask_user` selected-option highlighting with radio/checkbox glyphs; active/retained free-text answers are highlighted; option boxes grow vertically when text wraps; long answer chips wrap in the replay UI

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **`ask_user` follow-up question tool** — a new interaction primitive lets tools ask the user clarifying questions mid-run, rendered as stacked options with an explicit Submit button.
- **Configurable global defaults** — set a default persona and model preset applied to all new chats.
- **Kagi web search provider** — added as a selectable web search option.
- **Live thinking indicator** — shows an elapsed-time counter while the model reasons, including for Bedrock hidden/adaptive thinking.

### Bedrock & Thinking
- Restored visible streaming thinking for older models via a single thinking-mode classifier.
- Fixed Opus 4.8 thinking config rejection on both Bedrock and the direct API; added adaptive + effort thinking support.
- Transparent recovery from expired Bedrock credentials with clean messaging, and fixed a broken credential reset key.
- Sanitize MCP tool schemas to Bedrock's JSON Schema draft 2020-12 subset (robust allowlist-based sanitizer).

### Settings & UI
- Added collapsible tool sub-groups with persisted state in settings.
- Added output spillover toggle and threshold controls to the Tools settings UI.
- Fixed the thinking dropdown not refreshing when the model preset is switched in the popover.

### Bug Fixes & Polish
- Auto-return note content on `replace_in_note` / `write_note` errors, rendered on failed tool-call cards.
- Reverted the note→header wikilink autocomplete change.
- Reorganized `extensions/` under a new `examples/` top-level directory.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Templates integration** — `list_templates` and `apply_template` tools for Templater plugin interop (default disabled)
- **Webview tool** — open and interact with web content via a webview scaffold (aka Obsidian's built-in browser)
- **Output spillover** — long tool output automatically spills to temp files; configurable via `output_spillover_threshold` setting
- **on_approval_required hook** — new hook event with Promise.race approval pattern for external approval workflows
- **Backlinks & outlinks tools** — `get_backlinks` / `get_outlinks` tools with prompt enhancements to memory-search and memory-resolver
- **Bases query rendering** — `read_rendered_note` extension now renders Bases queries
- **Rendered Markdown preview** — toggle in diff view to preview rendered output

### Bug Fixes
- Fix task panel persisting across new conversation creation
- Unify note path resolution in path constraint enforcement

### Improvements
- **Per-panel persona isolation** — persona state is now scoped per panel in multi-panel mode
- **Stale tracker body hash** — bypass frontmatter-only changes for more accurate stale detection
- General refactoring across the code base

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Extended thinking support** — toggle thinking effort level per-preset with quick settings control
- **Task tracking tool** — internal `update_tasks` tool for AI task tracking during conversations
- **Command-pattern auto-approve** — granular approval rules for `execute_command`
- **Community extensions gallery** — browse and install extension tools (e.g., Dataview `read_rendered_note`)
- **Unicode fuzzy matching** — `replace_in_note` and `replace_in_file` handle Unicode normalization differences
- **Multi-engine web search** — extracted as an extension tool; built-in search simplified to DDG-only
- **Popover reference system** — inline superscript citations in assistant messages
- **Plan mode annotations** — write tools show [Act mode only] suffix in system prompt
- **Timestamp filtering** — `modified_after`/`modified_before` params for `search_vault` and `list_vault`
- **Auto-context expansion** — includes non-markdown open file types
- **Attachment rule triggers** — directory/tag rules now fire on attached notes, not just tool-accessed ones

### Bug Fixes
- Fix MCP tools not appearing in system prompt due to initialization race
- Fix erroneous compaction triggering and token footer overcounting
- Re-render approval buttons when switching back to conversation awaiting approval
- Re-resolve note path after hook delay using live TFile reference

### UI & Polish
- Thinking blocks included in HTML export
- Chevron indicator and scrollable height limit for task panel
- Notice shown when duplicate images are skipped during docx import

### Settings & Infrastructure
- Rename workflow trigger "scheduled" → "on-schedule" with unified Automation settings UI
- Auto-reconnect for MCP servers after system sleep/wake
- `utils.notify()` convenience method for extension authors

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Auto-inject workflow frontmatter** — Automatically injects workflow-specific frontmatter when creating new notes that match a workflow
- **Per-workflow model preset override** — Workflows can now specify a custom AI model preset
- **Per-workflow conversation mode override** — Workflows can override the default conversation mode
- **Per-hook execution delay with debounce semantics** — Workflow hooks support configurable execution delays with debounce behavior
- **Background workflow independence** — Removed orchestrator dependency for background workflows, enabling standalone execution

### Bug Fixes
- Fix workflow not appearing in Settings UI immediately after creation
- Fix case-insensitive directory handling for workflow discovery and creation
- Migrate workflow identification from `notor-workflow` to `notor-type: workflow` frontmatter key

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Delete any provider** — All providers (including the 4 defaults) can now be removed from settings. A confirmation dialog prevents accidental deletion, and the last remaining provider cannot be removed.
- **Rename any provider** — Display name is now editable for all providers, not just user-added ones. The section heading updates immediately on blur.

### Bug Fixes
- **Model presets now list all provider instances** — The provider dropdown in model presets and the chat view preset selector now shows individual provider instances with their custom display names, instead of a hardcoded list of provider types.
- **Fix provider type-vs-ID mismatches** — Multiple subsystems were still using provider type strings ("anthropic", "openai", etc.) where instance IDs are now expected:
  - Media capabilities (PDF handling, image support) now correctly resolve the active provider's type
  - Persona manager correctly switches providers and reverts to defaults using instance IDs
  - MCP hub image capability checks resolve the provider type from the active instance
  - Chat view "Custom" mode fallback uses the configured active provider instead of hardcoded "local"

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Multi-instance provider support** — Configure multiple instances of the same provider type (e.g., multiple OpenAI-compatible endpoints) with seamless migration from single-instance configs
- **PDF attachment support** — Extract and inline PDF text content at attach time so the AI receives full document context without tool calls; also extracts embedded images from PDFs using rendered page analysis with a 10MB size budget
- **Streaming Markdown rendering** — Render Markdown incrementally during LLM streaming for a more responsive chat experience

### Bug Fixes
- Fix external file attachment silently failing on PDFs; now includes source path in AI context
- Fix provider picker test to correctly select Custom mode before asserting provider select state
- Remove redundant persona picker from quick settings popover

### Improvements
- Implement preset-first fallback chain for conversation model restore (more reliable model selection on conversation load)
- Rename `provider_type`/`providerType` to `provider_id`/`providerId` across codebase for clarity with multi-instance support

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Import .docx files into vault** — New built-in `import_docx` tool lets you batch-import `.docx` documents directly into your Obsidian vault.
- **Attach .docx and other file types in chat** — The chat attachment picker now supports `.docx` files and an "open file type" option for broader file selection.

### Bug Fixes
- **Web search provider settings** — Provider-specific settings are now hidden when the web search API key is not configured, avoiding confusing options in the UI.
- **String option filtering** — Existing `string[]` settings entries are now filtered against the constrained option list at render time, preventing stale/invalid values from appearing.
- **Allowed paths bug fix** — Fixed a bug in the allowed-paths logic for the attachment picker.

### UI Polish
- **Web search provider visibility** — Re-render behavior in `ToolSettingsModal` is now correctly wired to `onSecretChange`, ensuring provider options appear/disappear promptly when the API key is added or removed.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Memory approval UX** — new bulk and inline approval modes for reviewing captured memories before they are saved; includes a dedicated approval panel with diff highlighting
- **`capture_memory` tool visibility** — the tool now appears in the Tools settings panel when memory is enabled, giving users direct control over memory capture behavior
- **`notor-last-recalled-at` timestamp** — memory notes now track when they were last recalled by the assistant, in addition to when they were created/updated
- **Auto-reload extensions and personas on file change** — extensions and personas reload automatically when their source files change on disk; parse/compile errors no longer clobber existing loaded state

### Settings & UI
- **Chat input height settings** — switched from sliders to validated text inputs for configuring minimum and maximum chat input height
- **Draft preservation** — chat input drafts are now preserved across sessions
- **Paste-resize fix** — pasting content into the chat input no longer causes unexpected resize jumps

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed `capture_memory` approval routing so memories are correctly routed for review
- Fixed approval buttons being hidden below the chat panel scroll boundary
- Fixed `capture_memory` tool invocation in the memory approval E2E test

### Memory Timestamps
- Split `notor-updated-at` into three targeted frontmatter timestamps for finer-grained memory tracking

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Find in Messages** — New command to search through messages in Notor chat panels
- **Copy & Context Menu** — Chat panel now has copy affordances and an enriched right-click context menu
- **AWS Bedrock Marketplace Models** — Notor now exposes marketplace Bedrock models via a dual-query `ListFoundationModels` lookup
- **Persona File Auto-Refresh** — Persona files are automatically reloaded when changed on disk, with a debounced notice
- **Settings Tools & Built-in Personas** — Added agent tools for editing Notor settings, a set of built-in personas, and tool creator guidance

### Bug Fixes
- Ensured the memory dedup-cache directory exists before atomic writes

### Settings & Providers UI
- Provider sub-settings are now grouped into unified cards in the Providers section
- Models settings section has improved visual separation with a cleaner card layout

### UI Polish
- Activity indicator is now global across all chat panels
- Memory recall visual indicator and block ordering fixed
- Removed redundant HR dividers and card borders from Models preset settings
- Collapsed per-item margins and internal dividers inside preset cards for a unified look

### Internals
- Added silent mode for note-open side effects suppressed in background sub-agents
- Added E2E test for find-in-messages

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes

- **Auto-approved tools no longer show Approve/Reject buttons** — Fixed a UI bug where auto-approved tool calls still rendered orphaned Approve/Reject buttons. The `renderApprovalPrompt()` method now accepts an `autoApproved` parameter and returns early when true, applied across all four fallback paths in `renderDiffApprovalPrompt()`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features

- **Knowledge Memory** — Full memory system allowing the assistant to capture, recall, and reason over user-specific knowledge across conversations. Includes a memory library, sub-agent profiles, built-in tools, automation scaffolds, settings UI with master toggle and auto-approval, and system prompt integration (Phases 0–10).
- **Extension Chat Blocks** — New primitive for rendering custom block types in the chat view. Extensions can emit styled, collapsible blocks via `utils.chatBlocks.emit()`. Includes rate limiting, sub-agent spawning, and persistence across reloads (Phases 1–13).
- **Template Variable Resolution** — Dynamic `{{variables}}` in system prompts and scaffolds, resolved at assembly time. Includes a registry, plugin-wide wiring, and a settings UI button to customize the system prompt (Phases 1–5).

### Bug Fixes

- Fix atomic write pattern in dedup cache and dream cursor to handle Obsidian's rename behavior
- Fix `write_docx` inline formatting, blockquotes, nested lists, and code blocks
- Fix sub-agent profile validation warnings by deferring `refreshVisibleProfiles` until after extension tools are registered
- Fix MCP tool mode classification not reflecting until Obsidian reload
- Fix title generation LLM prompt to prevent refusal responses

### UI Polish

- Always-visible persona chip, header favorite icon, expanded title width, and fix workflow double-slash

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Conversation title in chat header** — The active conversation title is now displayed in the chat panel header, with context menu and inline editing support
- **Model list refresh button** — Added a refresh button to reload available models without restarting the plugin

### Improvements
- **Persona UX overhaul** — Chip customisation, creation dropdown, click-to-switch behavior, and open-in-new-leaf support
- **Persona chip reactivity** — Fixed persona chip not updating when switching personas; improved switch UX
- **Attachment menu positioning** — More robust positioning logic for the attachment dropdown

### Bug Fixes
- **Favorites no longer pinned to top** — Favorited conversations are no longer forcibly pinned to the top of the history list, restoring natural sort order

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Side conversations (`/btw`)** — Fork the current conversation into a new panel for quick tangential discussions without losing context
- **Conversation deep-linking** — Link directly to specific conversations from vault notes, with chat history tools for navigating past conversations

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed conversation deep-links not working when triggered from vault notes
- Deep-links now open in a new tab instead of replacing the current panel

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Built-in sleep tool** — cancellable long-duration waits (e.g., waiting on backfill processes), with configurable max duration and poll interval
- **Tool search/filter bar** — fuzzy search across built-in, user, and MCP tools by name, description, and server name in Settings
- **Conversation rename** — right-click any conversation in the history list to rename it
- **Drag-to-resize chat input** — vertically resize the input text box; resets on send
- **Configurable tool approval timeout** — auto-skip unapproved tool calls after a user-defined timeout (default: no timeout)
- **MCP annotation notice** — warns when an MCP server's tools lack `readOnlyHint` annotations; right-click the notice to jump to that server's tool settings

### Bug Fixes
- **Per-conversation persona scoping** — persona selection is now scoped to each panel instead of toggling globally across all open panels; fixes chip not rendering on first load in stacked mode
- **Auto-clear expired AWS credentials** — detects expired security token errors and auto-clears cached Bedrock clients so the next request uses fresh credentials without restarting Obsidian
- **Persist annotation notice state** — the MCP annotation notice now fires once per server ever, not on every reload

### Improvements
- **Favorite retention protection** — favorited conversations are never pruned by auto-cleanup (age-based or size-based); retention runs on startup
- **TypeScript clean build** — fixed all pre-existing `tsc --noEmit` errors (`skipLibCheck` in tsconfig, fixed `mammoth.d.ts` default export)
- **Remove default Local Endpoint URL** — new users start with an empty field instead of a placeholder URL

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Model Presets & LLM Title Generation
- Added model presets system with core types, preset resolver, and settings migration
- Replaced provider+model dropdowns with a unified preset selector in chat panel
- Added Models settings section and renamed Provider setup to Providers
- Preset resolution now respects persona provider/model overrides
- Preset name is stored per conversation with preset-aware lifecycle switching
- Removed redundant Active Provider dropdown from settings UI

### Built-in Automations
- Added `on_conversation_start` trigger with metadata API, automation scaffolds, and `llmCall` helper
- Built-in automations are now user-customizable via vault files (matching the built-in tools pattern)
- Added title generation settings UI and filter scaffold automations from user list
- Centralized automation dispatch with `utils.llmCall` and `utils.conversationApi`
- Redesigned automations settings UI with row-based layout, toggles, and gear modal
- Fixed title generation automation failures (scaffold overrides, settings resolution, dispatch chain)
- Fixed session header writes clobbering automation-set titles
- Fixed conversation list UI not updating when title is set by automation

### Multi-Conversation Robustness Redesign
- Implemented orchestrator registry and factory infrastructure (Phase A)
- Extracted major subsystems from ChatOrchestrator: MessagePipeline, ConfigResolver, HookDispatcher, CompactionManager, ViewRouter, SessionManager, ConversationLifecycleManager, WorkflowExecutor (Phase B)
- Added cross-orchestrator session guard, persistence flush for JSONL write safety, and view close lifecycle
- Deleted obsolete `getOrchestrator()` and `createSecondaryOrchestrator()` methods

### MCP & Tools Settings
- Added MCP server wildcard syntax (`serverName__*`) to `notor_tool_config`
- Added collapsible tool descriptions in Settings for long text (>255 chars)
- Applied description truncation to MCP server tool discovery list
- Improved Settings UI: moved chevron before description text, aligned descriptions, made text block clickable

### UI & UX
- Added right-click context menu and middle-click to New Conversation button
- Added background note opening and promoted General settings to top-level section
- Fixed new chat panel loading most recent conversation instead of starting fresh
- Fixed model provider/ID split-brain between per-orchestrator and global ProviderRegistry state

### Documentation
- Added BRAT installation instructions as recommended install method
- Revised README to remove roadmap phases and reorganize features into clear categories

### Testing
- Added comprehensive E2E tests for phases A and B of the robustness redesign
- Added E2E test for title generation automation dispatch and execution
- Fixed multiple flaky and broken E2E tests

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

- **Fix multi-panel routing:** Restored primary orchestrator view after secondary panel creation, preventing the main panel from losing its view state
- **Fix send button stuck state:** Resolved issue where the send button would get stuck when switching conversations during active streaming

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **User-defined extensions system** — Create custom tools and automations via TypeScript scaffold files in your vault. Includes discovery, compilation, settings UI, and hot-reload.
- **Built-in tool migration to extension system** — All built-in tools (read_note, write_note, search_vault, web_search, etc.) now run through the unified extension scaffold pipeline, enabling per-tool settings, enable/disable toggles, and auto-approve controls.
- **Multi-panel chat** — Open multiple chat panels side-by-side, each with its own independent conversation session, orchestrator, and MCP serialization.
- **Multi-provider web search** — Pluggable web search backend with support for DuckDuckGo, Tavily, Brave Search, and SerpApi. Includes a per-provider rate-limiting queue (TaskLaneQueue).
- **PDF & image handling** — Attach PDFs and images to conversations. PDFs processed via unpdf (native + text extraction). Images supported in read_file, attachment picker, and DOCX round-tripping.
- **Conversation favorites** — Star/unstar conversations via context menu; filter to show only favorites.
- **"Launch active note workflow" command** — New command palette entry to pick and run a workflow with `{active_note}` template substitution.
- **"Open in new tab" for conversations** — Right-click a conversation in history to open it in a new panel.
- **write_file tool** — New built-in tool for writing arbitrary text files to the filesystem.
- **replace_in_file tool** — New built-in tool for SEARCH/REPLACE block editing on filesystem files.

### Improvements
- **Thread-safe streaming architecture** — Per-conversation session isolation (ConversationSession), tool policy extraction, dispatcher split, and JSONL history restoration.
- **Token counting fixes** — Fixed Anthropic input token bug, added diagnostic logging to all providers, real-time token footer updates, and token-based sub-agent limits with graceful wind-down.
- **Settings UI reorganization** — Extracted field renderer, tool settings modal, shared settings section, user automations renderer, and subsection deep-linking with highlight glow.
- **Session activity indicator** — Shows which conversation sessions are actively running in the foreground.
- **Sub-agent iteration cap** — Configurable max iterations setting (default 20) with token-based limits and auto-summary wind-down.
- **write_docx note_name parameter** — Convert vault notes directly to DOCX.
- **TaskLaneQueue** — Generic per-lane rate-limiting queue exposed to extensions via `utils.queue`.

### Bug Fixes
- Fix chat input stealing focus when LLM response completes
- Fix approval buttons targeting wrong tool call element during multi-tool turns
- Fix DOCX template grafting null crash
- Fix replace_in_note partial block accept/reject not being applied
- Fix auto-approve settings not propagating to new tools and subagents
- Fix tilde expansion in filesystem path handling
- Fix race condition: sync ToolDispatcher after async extension reload on boot
- Fix sub-agent tokens inflating compaction/truncation estimates
- Fix settings highlight deferred until element scrolls into view
- Fix user-defined tools/automations missing from Extensions Settings UI

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features

- **Sub-agents:** Full sub-agent system allowing the AI to spawn specialized child agents with scoped tool access, configurable profiles (built-in and user-defined), progress visibility, cancellation UX, and conversation history roll-up
- **Web search tool:** New `web_search` built-in tool with settings UI, system prompt guidance, and edge case handling
- **Conversation forking:** Fork any conversation from a specific message to explore alternate branches, with fork lineage badges and message ID tracking
- **Parallel tool execution:** Tools can now execute concurrently in batches, with grouped tool_call/tool_result message ordering and MCP tool opt-in for concurrent execution
- **Conversation delete:** Delete button added to conversation history list items

### Improvements

- **search_vault:** Added result limiting and pagination support
- **Settings deep-links:** Clickable deep-links in chat messages that open specific settings panels, with built-in `notor-help` sub-agent
- **Markdown tables:** Added subtle horizontal row separators for better readability
- **Conversation history UX:** Active conversation highlighting; history tab auto-closes on navigation actions
- **Settings UI:** Added `use_subagent`, `get_backlinks`, `get_outlinks`, and `web_search` to tool display names

### Bug Fixes

- Fixed BedrockProvider concurrency bug
- Fixed settings deep-links by attaching click handlers directly to link elements
- Fixed sub-agent Bedrock message format and discovery race condition
- Fixed directory-based rule trigger matching bug
- Fixed E2E test infrastructure issues across multiple test suites

### Documentation

- Added user documentation for sub-agents, export/import, vault tools, getting started, and safety
- Documented 5 previously undocumented tools, Bedrock setup, and commands

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **JSONL embedding in HTML exports** — HTML exports now embed conversation data as JSONL directly in the file, enabling portable backups that carry their own structured data
- **Conversation import from HTML** — Import conversations back into the vault from previously exported HTML files, reading the embedded JSONL data

### Testing
- Added E2E test coverage for HTML export JSONL embedding and conversation import flow

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Tools settings section** — Unified UI to globally enable/disable individual tools with per-tool auto-approve toggles and config override YAML snippets
- **Rules & workflows settings** — New settings section with Create buttons, trigger type dropdowns, and scoped inline name prompts for rules and workflows
- **Chat history search** — Search through past chat conversations
- **`search_vault` sort_by parameter** — Sort vault search results by relevance, modified date, or created date
- **Workflow aliases** — Slash command suggestor now recognizes workflow aliases

### Improvements
- **Cancel behavior overhaul** — Stop button now immediately unblocks the user; tools execute to completion, no orphaned `tool_use` errors, and conversation continues cleanly after cancel
- **`fetch_webpage` diagnostics** — Native fetch diagnostic probe on failure, Chromium net error hints in logs
- **1M extended context** — Support for Claude Opus 4.6 with 1M token context window
- **MCP tool config** — Copy tool config YAML snippets now include MCP tools; visual separator between Enabled and Auto-approve toggles

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed cancel during tool call causing orphaned `tool_calls` and blocked approval promises
- Fixed compaction test crash and race condition in cancel flow
- Fixed e2e test stability: mcp-http selectors, workflow-execution fixtures, interaction-test for contenteditable input, wikilink coexistence idle wait, workflow-discovery directory cleanup

### Documentation
- Updated docs for tool enable/disable, tool config overrides, workflow aliases, search_vault sort_by, chat history search, cancel behavior, and settings UI

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Chat history export** — Export conversations to Markdown or HTML format
- **Clickable vault note links** — Note references in chat messages are now clickable, opening directly in Obsidian
- **Vault note autocomplete** — Fuzzy search across note paths and aliases when referencing vault notes

### Bug Fixes
- **Plan mode error surfacing** — Error details are now shown to the LLM when tool calls are blocked in Plan mode
- **Copy tool config YAML** — Now correctly includes all built-in tools
- **Settings section state** — Expand/collapse state is properly preserved across redisplay

### UI Improvements
- Chat input icons moved below the input box with dynamic height expansion
- Reordered chat panel header icons
- Settings section expand/collapse state now persists across sessions
- MCP servers broken out as its own settings section

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Tools
- **`move_note`** — Rename or relocate notes within the vault, automatically preserving links
- **`get_backlinks` / `get_outlinks`** — Query the note link graph to discover connections between notes

### Bedrock Model Picker Overhaul
- Enriched model metadata with extended context (1M token) variant support
- Grouped model picker UI with 1M context variants
- Extended context runtime integration for seamless large-context conversations

### Tests
- Added e2e tests for `move_note`, `get_backlinks`/`get_outlinks`, Bedrock model picker, and LLM interaction

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **DOCX Comments Extraction** — New `extract_docx_comments` tool for extracting review comments from `.docx` files, with corresponding Settings UI configuration

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed Settings connection test success messages to be readable across all themes
- Fixed Settings connection test error messages to be readable, selectable, and logged
- Added `private/` directory to `.gitignore`

### ESLint Cleanup
- Resolved 20+ ESLint issues flagged by the Obsidian community plugin review, including:
  - Replaced deprecated APIs (`execCommand`, `Buffer.slice`, `noticeEl`)
  - Removed unnecessary async/await and unused imports
  - Replaced inline styles with CSS classes
  - Added proper void operators for floating promises
  - Enforced sentence case for UI text

### E2E Test Infrastructure
- Built shared test harness and migrated all e2e test scripts (Phase 1–3) to use it
- Stabilized multiple flaky tests (auto-context, auto-approve, persona, diff-approval, chat-scroll, and more)
- Updated test helpers for contenteditable input changes

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Feature: Tool Configuration via `<notor_tool_config>` Tags
A major new capability allowing workflow-level control over tool behavior through inline YAML configuration tags in system prompts.

- **Parsing & merging engine** — Parses `<notor_tool_config>` YAML blocks from workflow prompts with structured error reporting
- **Dispatcher & registry modifications** — Tools can be disabled, auto-approved, or path-restricted per-workflow
- **Pipeline integration** — Tool configs are extracted during workflow assembly and flow through the orchestrator to both `assemble()` and `sendMessage()`
- **Settings & Inspector UI** — New settings tab section and in-chat inspector for reviewing active tool configuration rules
- **Cross-reference validation** — Unit and integration tests covering parsing, merging, precedence, and enforcement

### Bug Fixes
- Aligned local ESLint config with Obsidian review bot requirements
- Made e2e test harness robust to Obsidian multi-page lifecycle

### Testing
- Added shared e2e test harness with 7 end-to-end test scripts covering tool-config parsing, disabled tools, auto-approve, path enforcement, precedence, inspector, and settings UI
- Comprehensive unit and integration test suite for the tool-config module

### Spec & Planning
- Full spec, implementation plan, and multiple rounds of risk audits (RT-1 through RT-6) resolving 30+ integration risks before implementation

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features
- **Note reference autocomplete**: Type `[[` in the chat input to get an autocomplete popup for linking to vault notes. Supports keyboard navigation (arrow keys + Tab/Enter to select).
- **Workflow inline tokens**: Workflows are now selected as inline tokens in the chat input (instead of chips), and pasted workflow references are parsed automatically.
- **Flexible note resolution**: All note tools now use a more robust note resolution strategy across the vault.
- **Focus chat on new conversation**: The chat input is automatically focused when starting a new conversation.

### Bug Fixes
- **Note autocomplete**: Tab key now correctly attaches the arrow-key-navigated suggestion (not always the first); popup closes immediately on selection; typed text is preserved on duplicate attachment; duplicate attachment no longer corrupts existing wikilink spans.
- **Attachment menu**: Fixed dropdown not appearing on button click; fixed allowed paths not being honoured and external file attachment being broken.
- **Workflow suggester**: Now picks up new/renamed notes immediately; prevents multiple workflow tokens in the chat input; improved slash-command suggest UX.
- **Compaction**: Fixed token estimation and truncation boundary safety; fixed pending messages not being re-appended after compaction (required for Bedrock); fixed optional chain in `extractPendingMessages`.
- **General**: Show a clear error when no model is selected.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes

- **Settings UI:** Fixed an issue where relative template paths were not resolved correctly against the vault root, causing template selection to fail in certain vault configurations.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New Features

#### DOCX File Support
- **Read plain text files** via a new `read_file` tool — supports `.txt`, `.md`, and other text formats from the vault or configured allowed paths
- **Read DOCX files** via a new `read_docx` tool — extracts text content from Word documents using the `mammoth` library
- **Write DOCX files** via a new `write_docx` tool — generates `.docx` files from a template using `pizzip` + `docx` templating, enabling AI-driven document creation

#### Inline Wikilink Tokens in Chat
- Vault notes attached to a message are now rendered as **inline wikilink-style tokens** (e.g. `[[Note Title]]`) directly within the chat input, replacing the previous chip-based attachment UI for vault notes
- Deleting a wikilink token in the editor properly **restores the attachment on Undo** (Ctrl/Cmd+Z)

#### Attachments UI Polish
- The `<attachments>` XML block sent to the AI is now rendered as a **collapsed `<details>` element** in the chat UI, keeping the conversation view clean while preserving full context visibility on expand

### Settings
- New **DOCX Tools settings section** in the plugin settings panel, allowing users to configure which tools are enabled and set allowed read/write paths for file access

### Bug Fixes
- Fixed attachment state not restoring correctly after wikilink token deletion via Undo
- Fixed `<attachments>` block rendering as raw XML in chat history

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Internal improvements

- Fixed async/floating-promise handling across several components to comply with stricter linting rules
  - Removed incorrect `async` modifier from `rescanWorkflows`
  - Wrapped async `removeBtn` click handler in a `void` IIFE
  - Prefixed floating-promise callbacks with `void` in `ConversationManager`

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug Fixes

- **MCP stdio server PATH injection** — Login shell PATH is now correctly injected when spawning stdio MCP servers, fixing failures where servers couldn't find executables (e.g. `node`, `python`, `npx`) that are only on the login shell PATH.

- **MCP error surfacing** — stderr output from stdio MCP servers is now captured and included in error messages, making it much easier to diagnose connection failures.

- **MCP info-level log capture** — Info-level log messages from MCP servers are now captured alongside errors, improving observability.

- **Stale McpHub settings reference** — Fixed a bug where McpHub was holding a stale reference to settings, which could cause incorrect behavior after settings were updated.

- **Expanded MCP debug logging** — More detailed debug logging has been added throughout the MCP connection lifecycle to aid troubleshooting.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Developer tooling

- **Obsidian ESLint plugin wired up** — `eslint-plugin-obsidianmd` is now correctly configured and running via `npm run lint`. Previously the plugin was installed but never actually loading due to a config bug (the `configs.recommended` rules object was being spread as config arguments instead of applied as rules).

- **TypeScript lint rules restored** — `@typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars` and `@typescript-eslint/no-require-imports` are now active, matching the disable comments already present in source files.

- **`fetch` global restricted** — `no-restricted-globals` now flags direct use of `fetch`, enforcing use of Obsidian's `requestUrl` for cross-platform compatibility. Existing intentional `fetch` uses in streaming inference calls are explicitly suppressed with comments explaining the rationale.

### Bug fixes / code quality

- Removed redundant plugin-name heading from the settings tab (`obsidianmd/settings-tab/no-problematic-settings-headings` — Obsidian already renders the plugin name above the settings panel).
- Removed unused imports: `StreamChunk` (compaction), `TFile` (vault-rules), `Setting` (settings-tab).
- Prefixed genuinely unused parameters with `_` in orchestrator, hook-events, and main.
- Removed now-unnecessary `eslint-disable` comments in history parser (destructured `_`-prefixed vars already satisfy the ignore pattern).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Code Quality Improvements
- **Promise handling** — Resolved floating and void-context Promise issues throughout the codebase
- **Removed spurious `async`** — Cleaned up methods that declared `async` but had no `await`
- **Type safety** — Replaced unsafe `as TFile`/`as TFolder` casts with proper `instanceof` narrowing; removed unnecessary type assertions and tightened `no-explicit-any` coverage

### Obsidian API Compliance
- **Settings UI** — Replaced raw HTML heading construction with the Obsidian `Setting` API
- **CSS** — Migrated inline `element.style.*` assignments to CSS classes and `setCssProps`
- **Config paths** — Replaced hardcoded `.obsidian` path references with `Vault#configDir`
- **Confirm dialogs** — Replaced native `confirm()` calls with an Obsidian Modal

### MCP Transport
- Migrated deprecated `SSEClientTransport` to `StreamableHTTPClientTransport`

### Other Fixes
- Fixed `String()` coercion of objects producing `[object Object]`
- Fixed ESLint disable comments to include required descriptions
- Removed unused variables and imports
- Fixed sentence case violations in UI-facing text
- Resolved disallowed `console` method usage

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix `manifest.json` description to align to submitted description to Obsidian community plugins

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Improvements
- **Settings UI overhaul:** MCP server settings are now organized into collapsible groups for a cleaner, less cluttered layout
- **Toggle switches:** MCP server enable/disable controls replaced with native Obsidian toggle switches (consistent with plugin conventions)
- **Status icons:** MCP connection status indicators now use Lucide icons instead of colored dots, improving accessibility
- **Configurable log level:** Added a minimum log level setting (default: `error`) to control console verbosity
- **Chat scroll behavior:** Fixed the chat panel fighting the user's scroll position when new content arrived

### Bug Fixes
- Settings section headings now use the Obsidian `setHeading()` API instead of raw HTML elements
- Replaced `document`/`window` globals with `activeDocument`/`activeWindow` for correct multi-window support
- Migrated `Vault.modify()` calls to `Vault.process()` to avoid race conditions on concurrent file writes
- Replaced `innerHTML` SVG assignments with `setIcon()` calls
- Fixed `toggleClass()` calls to always pass an explicit boolean second argument
- MCP status icons in Settings now update live via `onStatusChange` subscription instead of only on open

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Bug fixes

- **MCP server name field now accepts hyphens** — Typing a hyphenated server name like `my-server` in the Add MCP server form no longer strips the `-` on each keystroke. The field now performs a full cleanup only when you leave the field.
- **Shell-quoted arguments for MCP stdio servers** — The arguments field for stdio MCP servers now correctly handles quoted arguments containing spaces (e.g. `-y server "/path/with spaces"`). Previously, arguments were split on every space, breaking paths that contained spaces.
- **Chat error messages now visible across themes** — Error blocks in the chat panel were rendering as a blank red box with no visible text on some themes. The error text is now reliably readable in both dark and light themes.

### Improvements

- **AWS Bedrock connection test** — A "Test connection" button has been added to the Bedrock provider settings. It performs the equivalent of `aws sts get-caller-identity` and displays your Account ID and ARN in a notice, so you can confirm your credentials are working without leaving Obsidian.

### Debug

- **Bedrock stream errors now logged to console** — When a Bedrock streaming error occurs (e.g. `Truncated event message received`), the full raw error object is now dumped to the Obsidian developer console, making it easier to inspect the hidden `$response` field referenced in AWS SDK error messages.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.2.0 — Custom MCP Servers

Connect any MCP-compatible tool server to Notor and use its tools alongside built-in tools in your AI conversations.

### What's new

**Custom MCP server support**
- Register external tool servers in **Settings → Notor → MCP servers** using three transport types: stdio (local process), SSE, or Streamable HTTP
- Discovered tools are automatically registered and appear alongside built-in tools — no special prompting required
- All custom tools go through the same dispatch pipeline as built-in tools: Plan/Act enforcement, auto-approve checks, and the approval UI apply uniformly

**Plan/Act enforcement for MCP tools**
- Each MCP tool is classified as read-only or write, defaulting to the server-reported `readOnlyHint`
- Write-classified tools are blocked in Plan mode, just like built-in write tools
- Override classifications per-tool in Settings when a server's hints don't match your expectations
- The current Plan/Act mode is sent to MCP servers on every tool call via `_meta.notor_mode` so cooperative servers can act accordingly

**Per-tool auto-approve**
- Configure auto-approve on a per-tool basis for each MCP server
- Per-persona auto-approve overrides (approve/deny/global) extend to MCP tools
- Stale override entries are preserved with a warning when a server disconnects or a tool is removed

**MCP status indicator in the chat panel**
- Quick-access status indicator in the chat header shows the health of all configured servers at a glance
- Click to open a popover with per-server connection status and enable/disable toggles — no need to open Obsidian Settings

**Connection lifecycle management**
- stdio servers: process is spawned on enable, terminated on disable or plugin unload
- HTTP servers: automatic reconnection with exponential backoff on disconnection
- Server failures are isolated — a crashed or timed-out server does not affect built-in tools, other MCP servers, or the active conversation
- Plugin startup is non-blocking: MCP connections are established in the background while the chat panel is immediately usable

**Security & trust**
- Prominent trust warnings when adding any new server
- Additional notice for stdio servers that a local process will be spawned with your user permissions
- Sensitive stdio environment variable values and HTTP headers can be stored via Obsidian's secrets manager
- stdio transport is desktop-only (not available on mobile)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## 0.1.0 — Initial pre-release

Notor's first packaged release. A transparent, extensible, autonomous-capable AI assistant for Obsidian — currently desktop-only.

### Chat & LLM integration
- Side-panel chat UI with streaming token-by-token responses
- **Four LLM providers**: Local/OpenAI-compatible (Ollama, LM Studio), AWS Bedrock (with SSO/profile support), Anthropic API, OpenAI API
- Dynamic model selection and provider switching from the chat panel
- Configurable system prompt with vault-file override (`{notor_dir}/prompts/core-system-prompt.md`)
- Plan mode (read-only) and Act mode (full tool access) toggle

### Vault tools
- `read_note`, `write_note`, `replace_in_note` — read, create, and surgically edit notes
- `search_vault`, `list_vault` — regex search and directory listing
- `read_frontmatter`, `update_frontmatter`, `manage_tags` — structured metadata operations
- `fetch_webpage` — fetch and convert web pages to Markdown (with domain denylist)
- `execute_command` — cross-platform shell command execution
- Diff preview UI with per-change accept/reject
- Per-tool auto-approve settings

### Context & intelligence
- File/note attachment picker with `[[wikilink]]` autocomplete
- Auto-context injection (open notes, vault structure)
- Auto-compaction for long conversations
- Checkpoints with snapshot/restore
- Chat history persistence (JSONL)
- Vault-level rule files for conditional context injection
- `<include_note>` tag for dynamic note content injection

### Workflows & personas
- Workflow notes with frontmatter-driven triggers, scheduling, and persona assignment
- Vault event hooks (on-save, on-tag-change, on-schedule, on-note-open)
- LLM lifecycle hooks (pre-send, on-tool-call, on-tool-result, after-completion)
- File-based persona system with per-persona model preferences and system prompts
- Per-persona auto-approve overrides
- Workflow activity indicator and concurrency management
- `/workflow` slash command for discovery and execution

### Infrastructure
- Credential storage via Obsidian's secrets manager API
- Token and cost tracking per message/conversation
- GitHub Actions release workflow
